pattern: Build a map of the six HTML entities and scan the string left-to-right, replacing each valid entity sequence found while leaving unrecognized ampersand sequences intact.
hints: Create a lookup table mapping each entity string (including the surrounding & and ;) to its replacement character.
Scan character by character; when you encounter '&', check if the next characters form a known entity and replace if so.
Entities can appear adjacent to each other or in the middle of regular text — walk through replacing greedily from left to right.

edge_inputs.description: invalid entity that looks like a valid prefix
no entities at all, plain text
partial entity with no closing semicolon

stress.description: random mix of valid entities and plain text
